task_completion.description: Agent executes network analysis tools to identify active ports and protocols.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing commands like 'netstat', 'ss', or 'lsof' being run, ideally revealing UDP ports 7400/7401 associated with the OpenICE application.

task_completion.description: Agent inspects build configuration files to identify the DDS middleware and dependencies.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the contents of 'build.gradle' files within the /opt/openice/mdpnp directory, revealing dependencies like nddsjava.jar, log4j, or Spring.

task_completion.description: Agent searches the source code for authentication and encryption controls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing search commands (e.g., 'grep -i auth', 'grep -i encrypt') or a text editor viewing Java source files within /opt/openice/mdpnp to evaluate security controls.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the security assessment report at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) is open, and the window title, save dialog, or status bar clearly shows the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/openice_security_assessment.txt'.

task_completion.description: The report includes Network Analysis and Supply Chain sections with specific findings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the report containing sections for Network and Supply Chain, explicitly mentioning 'DDS', 'UDP' or 'TCP', specific port numbers (e.g., 7400+), and identified dependencies (e.g., RTI Connext, nddsjava, log4j).

task_completion.description: The report includes Authentication, Risk, and Recommendation sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the report containing sections for Authentication, Risk, and Recommendations, discussing the presence/absence of encryption/auth controls and including keywords like 'finding' and 'severity'.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually performed the analysis rather than hallucinating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent actively running network commands and inspecting source/build files in the terminal or editor before writing the report.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent manually constructed the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is seen being written or edited in a standard text editor (GUI or terminal-based like nano/vim), rather than being generated by a single automated script or 'echo' command.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just copy-paste generic prompt terms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final report contains specific technical details discovered during the task (e.g., port 7400, nddsjava.jar, Gradle, specific Java versions) that were not explicitly provided in the initial task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenICE uses RTI Connext DDS middleware (nddsjava.jar). Default network ports are UDP 7400 (discovery) and 7401 (user data). The build system is Gradle (build.gradle files). Key dependencies include log4j, slf4j, Spring Framework, MongoDB driver, HAPI HL7/FHIR, and JavaFX. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/openice_security_assessment.txt and contain sections: Network, Supply Chain, Authentication, Risk, Recommendation. Critical keywords to look for in the report: DDS, UDP, TCP, port, finding, severity.
